feature.name: Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode.
feature.creator: sejalanand@microsoft.com
feature.summary:
This change updates Chromium’s emoji rendering behavior in Forced Colors Mode. During computed-value resolution, emoji whose font-variant-emoji value computes to normal or unicode are rendered using their monochrome glyphs when available.

Chromium will therefore suppress color emoji rendering, which ensures emojis fully participate in the Forced Colors Mode pipeline and respect system high-contrast colors. Behavior outside forced colors mode is unchanged.

intake.finding:
ChromeStatus record (created 2025-11-24 by sejalanand@microsoft.com). This change updates Chromium’s emoji rendering behavior in Forced Colors Mode. During computed-value resolution, emoji whose font-variant-emoji value computes to normal or unicode are rendered using their monochrome glyphs when available.

Chromium will therefore suppress color emoji rendering, which ensures emojis fully participate in the Forced Colors Mode pipeline and respect system high-contrast colors. Behavior outside forced colors mode is unchanged. Category: Miscellaneous. Blink component: Blink>Fonts. Stars: 85.

adoption.finding: The WebDX/WebFeature counter 'ForcedColors' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 165) measured 35.3797% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2024-11-10 with 563 data points. Mapping confidence: declared-family.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures the ForcedColors family/property, not this specific feature. Does not isolate the Chrome 145 increment.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.
